ConstructorArguments: Dispatch this compute shader using explicit thread counts.

Documentation.Summary: Dispatch this compute shader using explicit thread counts.
Documentation.Remarks:
The specified thread counts will be automatically divided by the thread group size
defined in the shader to compute the final dispatch group count.
<para>
When called outside a graphics context, the dispatch runs immediately.  
When called inside a graphics context, the dispatch runs async.
</para>

Params.threadsX: The number of threads to dispatch in the X dimension.
Params.threadsY: The number of threads to dispatch in the Y dimension.
Params.threadsZ: The number of threads to dispatch in the Z dimension.

ConstructorArguments: Dispatch this compute shader by reading thread group counts (x, y, z) from an indirect buffer of type Sandbox.GpuBuffer.IndirectDispatchArguments.

Documentation.Summary:
Dispatch this compute shader by reading thread group counts (x, y, z)
from an indirect buffer of type <see cref="T:Sandbox.GpuBuffer.IndirectDispatchArguments" />.
Documentation.Remarks:
<para><paramref name="indirectBuffer" /> must be created with <see cref="F:Sandbox.GpuBuffer.UsageFlags.IndirectDrawArguments" />  
            and have an element size of 12 bytes.
            </para><para><paramref name="indirectElementOffset" /> is an element index into <paramref name="indirectBuffer" />, not a byte offset.
            </para><para>
            When called outside a graphics context, the dispatch runs immediately.  
            When called inside a graphics context, the dispatch runs async.
            </para>

Params.indirectBuffer: The GPU buffer containing one or more dispatch argument entries.
Params.indirectElementOffset: The index of the dispatch arguments element to use (each element = 12 bytes).

Documentation.Summary: Create a compute shader from the specified path.

ConstructorArguments: Attributes that are passed to the compute shader on dispatch.

Documentation.Summary: Attributes that are passed to the compute shader on dispatch.

ConstructorArguments: A compute shader is a program that runs on the GPU, often with data provided to/from the CPU by means of a GpuBuffer or a Texture.

Documentation.Summary: A compute shader is a program that runs on the GPU, often with data provided to/from the CPU by means of a <see cref="T:Sandbox.GpuBuffer`1" /> or a <see cref="T:Sandbox.Texture" />.

ConstructorArguments: Destroys the GPU buffer, don't use it no more

Documentation.Summary: Destroys the GPU buffer, don't use it no more

ConstructorArguments: Retrieves the GPU buffer and copies them into a provided Span.
Retrieves a number of elements from the GPU buffer and copies them into a provided Span.
Asynchronously retrieves data from the GPU buffer and provides it to the callback.
Asynchronously retrieves a number of elements from the GPU buffer and provides it to the callback.
Synchronously uploads data from a Span to the GPU, replacing the existing data in this GpuBuffer.
Synchronously uploads data from a List to the GPU, replacing the existing data in this GpuBuffer.

For Sandbox.GpuBuffer.UsageFlags.Append buffers there is a hidden uint 32-bit atomic counter in the buffer that contains the number of writes to the buffer after invocation of the compute shader. In order to get the value of the counter, the data needs to be copied to another GPU buffer that can be used.

Documentation.Summary:
For <see cref="F:Sandbox.GpuBuffer.UsageFlags.Append" /> buffers there is a hidden uint 32-bit atomic counter in the buffer that contains the number of 
writes to the buffer after invocation of the compute shader.  In order to get the value of the counter, the data needs to be copied to
another GPU buffer that can be used.

ConstructorArguments: Fills the entire buffer with a repeated uint32 value. Uses the native GPU fill command (vkCmdFillBuffer) — no CPU-side allocation needed.

Documentation.Summary:
Fills the entire buffer with a repeated uint32 value.
Uses the native GPU fill command (vkCmdFillBuffer) — no CPU-side allocation needed.

Params.value: The uint32 value to fill with. Defaults to zero.

ConstructorArguments: Sets the counter value for Sandbox.GpuBuffer.UsageFlags.Append or Sandbox.GpuBuffer.UsageFlags.Counter structured buffers.

Documentation.Summary: Sets the counter value for <see cref="F:Sandbox.GpuBuffer.UsageFlags.Append" /> or <see cref="F:Sandbox.GpuBuffer.UsageFlags.Counter" /> structured buffers.
Creates a new GPU buffer with a specified number of elements and a specific buffer type.

Params.elementCount: The total number of elements that the GpuBuffer can hold. This represents the buffer's size in terms of elements, not bytes.
Params.elementSize: The total number of elements that the GpuBuffer can hold. This represents the buffer's size in terms of elements, not bytes.
Params.flags: Defines the usage pattern of the GPU buffer. This can affect performance depending on how the buffer is utilized.

ConstructorArguments: Number of elements in the buffer.

Documentation.Summary: Number of elements in the buffer.

ConstructorArguments: Size of a single element in the buffer.

Documentation.Summary: Size of a single element in the buffer.

ConstructorArguments: What sort of buffer this is

Documentation.Summary: What sort of buffer this is

ConstructorArguments: A GPU data buffer intended for use with a ComputeShader. You can read and write arbitrary data to and from the CPU and GPU. This allows for efficient parallel data processing on the GPU. Different GPU buffer types can be used depending on the provided Sandbox.GpuBuffer.UsageFlags. Using the default Sandbox.GpuBuffer.UsageFlags.Structured type buffers map to StructuredBuffer<T> and RWStructuredBuffer<T> in HLSL.

A GPU data buffer intended for use with a ComputeShader. You can read and write arbitrary data to and from the CPU and GPU. This allows for efficient parallel data processing on the GPU. Different GPU buffer types can be used depending on the provided Sandbox.GpuBuffer.UsageFlags. Using the default Sandbox.GpuBuffer.UsageFlags.Structured type buffers map to StructuredBuffer<T> and RWStructuredBuffer<T> in HLSL.

Documentation.Summary:
A GPU data buffer intended for use with a <see cref="T:Sandbox.ComputeShader" />.

You can read and write arbitrary data to and from the CPU and GPU.
This allows for efficient parallel data processing on the GPU.

Different GPU buffer types can be used depending on the provided <see cref="T:Sandbox.GpuBuffer.UsageFlags" />.
Using the default <see cref="F:Sandbox.GpuBuffer.UsageFlags.Structured" /> type buffers map to StructuredBuffer<T> and RWStructuredBuffer<T> in HLSL.

ConstructorArguments: Can be used as a vertex buffer.

Documentation.Summary: Can be used as a vertex buffer.

ConstructorArguments: Can be used as an index buffer.

Documentation.Summary: Can be used as an index buffer.
Byte Address Buffer (HLSL RWByteAddressBuffer)
Structured Buffer (HLSL RWStructuredBuffer)
Append Structured Buffer (HLSL AppendStructuredBuffer)

ConstructorArguments: Structured and Append buffers automatically have counters
Indirect argument buffer for indirect draws <seealso></seealso><seealso></seealso>

Documentation.Summary:
Indirect argument buffer for indirect draws
<seealso cref="T:Sandbox.GpuBuffer.IndirectDrawArguments" /><seealso cref="T:Sandbox.GpuBuffer.IndirectDrawIndexedArguments" />
You can combine these e.g UsageFlags.Index | UsageFlags.ByteAddress for a buffer that can be used as an index buffer and in a compute shader.
Number of vertices to draw per instance.
Number of instances to draw.
Index of the first vertex to draw.
Instance ID of the first instance.
Number of indices to draw per instance.
Number of instances to draw.
Index of the first index to draw.
Value added to each index before indexing into the vertex buffer.
Instance ID of the first instance.
A typed GpuBuffer

TypeParams.T:
The type of data that the GpuBuffer will store.
Must be a <see href="https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/framework/interop/blittable-and-non-blittable-types">blittable</see> value type.
